LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT HARVEY KLYCE'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION. DISCUSSED. FOR GOOD CAUSE SHOWN AND PER AGREEMENT OF COUNSEL, THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND SET THE FOLLOWING BRIEFING SCHEDULE: RESPONDENT'S SUPPLEMENT BRIEF IS DUE ON 11/20/17; OPPOSITION BRIEF IS DUE ON 12/11/17; REPLY BRIEF IS DUE ON 12/18/17; HEARING IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 21, 2017 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. PARTIES ARE TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR PLEADINGS TO DEPARTMENT 302. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER CSR #6576 EMAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, RESPONDENT HARVEY ROBERT KLYCE'S PETITION TO VACATE THE ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED. MORGAN STANLEY SMITH BARNEY, LLC AND MORGAN STANLEY SMITH BARNEY FA NOTES HOLDING, LLC'S PETITION TO CONFIRM THE ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. THE COURT ORDERS THE ARBITRATION AWARD CONFIRMED PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTIONS 1285.2, 1286, AND 1286.2. MR. KLYCE FAILED TO SATISFY THE REQUIREMENTS UNDER C.C.P. SEC. 1286.2 TO VACATE AN AWARD. HE DID NOT SHOW THAT HIS RIGHTS WERE SUBSTANTIALLY PREJUDICED BY ARBITRATOR MISCONDUCT. HE ALSO FAILED TO SHOW HOW THE ARBITRATORS EXCEEDED THEIR POWERS, EXCLUDED MATERIAL EVIDENCE, OR FAILED TO GRANT A CONTINUANCE REQUEST. THE AWARD DID NOT VIOLATE FINRA RULES. MR. KLYCE FAILED TO SHOW THAT THE ARBITRATORS LACKED AUTHORITY TO ADJUDICATE HIS CLAIMS UNDER THE UNIFORMED SERVICES EMPLOYMENT AND REEMPLOYMENT RIGHTS ACT ("USERRA"). "CONSISTENT WITH THE OTHER CIRCUITS TO HAVE CONSIDERED THE QUESTION, WE THEREFORE CONCLUDE THAT THE PLAIN TEXT OF USERRA DOES NOT PRECLUDE THE COMPELLED ARBITRATION OF DISPUTES ARISING UNDER ITS PROVISIONS." (ZIOBER V. BLB RESOURCES, INC. (2016) 839 F.3D 814, 820.) MOREOVER, MR. KLYCE SUBMITTED TO ARBITRATION AND FILED A COUNTERCLAIM. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) COUNSEL FOR PETITIONERS SUBMITS A PROPOSED ORDER AND PROPOSED JUDGMENT. JUDGE: ANDREW CHENG, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR# 6576, RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG, REPORTED. (D302/AC)

Second Corrected Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Dec-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Nicholas Fram, Esq. 415-512-4060 of Munger, Toiles & Olsen LLP for Petitioner.
No oher Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. Petitioner discusses fees and judgment. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER MORGAN STANLEY SMITH BARNEY, LLC, MORGAN STANLEY SMITH BARNEY FA NOTES HOLDINGS, LLC's Second Corrected Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award.

Morgan Stanley Smith Barney, LLC and Morgan Stanley Smith Barney FA Notes Holding, LLC's petition to confirm arbitration award is granted for the reasons stated in the companion motion to vacate.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript) Counsel for Petitioners submits a proposed order and proposed judgment.

Judge: Andrew Cheng, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR# 6576, rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/AC)

Notice Of Petition To Vacate Arb
Off Calendar Dec-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Nicholas Fram, Esq. 415-512-4060 of Munger, Toiles & Olsen LLP for Petitioner.
No oher Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. Petitioner discusses fees and judgment. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT HARVEY KLYCE's Petition To Vacate Arbitration Award.

Respondent Harvey Robert Klyce's petition to vacate the arbitration award is denied. Morgan Stanley Smith Barney, LLC and Morgan Stanley Smith Barney FA Notes Holding, LLC's petition to confirm the arbitration award is granted. The Court orders the arbitration award confirmed pursuant to C.C.P. Sections 1285.2, 1286, and 1286.2. Mr. Klyce failed to satisfy the requirements under C.C.P. Sec. 1286.2 to vacate an award. He did not show that his rights were substantially prejudiced by arbitrator misconduct. He also failed to show how the arbitrators exceeded their powers, excluded material evidence, or failed to grant a continuance request. The award did not violate FINRA rules. Mr. Klyce failed to show that the arbitrators lacked authority to adjudicate his claims under the Uniformed Services Employment and Reemployment Rights Act ("USERRA"). "Consistent with the other circuits to have considered the question, we therefore conclude that the plain text of USERRA does not preclude the compelled arbitration of disputes arising under its provisions." (Ziober v. BLB Resources, Inc. (2016) 839 F.3d 814, 820.) Moreover, Mr. Klyce submitted to arbitration and filed a counterclaim. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript) Counsel for Petitioners submits a proposed order and proposed judgment.

Judge: Andrew Cheng, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR# 6576, rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/AC)

Notice Of Petition To Vacate Arb
Off Calendar Oct-10-2017 Continued To Dec-21-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Harvey Klyce, Tel. 415.887.8402, respondent and moving party appearing on his own behalf;
Bryan H. Heckenlively, Esq. and Nicholas D. Fram, Esq. of Munger tolles & Olson LLP, Tel. 415.512.4077/4060, for petitioner and opposing party.

RULING: At the start of the hearing, Respondent Harvey Klyce requested the Court for additional time to find representation and file a supplemental brief. The matter was discussed. For good cause shown and per agreement of counsel for petitioner, the Court vacated its tentative ruling and set the following briefing schedule on RESPONDENT HARVEY KLYCE's Petition To Vacate Arbitration: Respondent's supplement brief is due on 11/20/17; Opposition brief is due on 12/11/17; Reply brief is due on 12/18/17; Hearing is continued to December 21, 2017 at 9:30 am in Department 302. Parties are to provide courtesy copies of their pleadings to Department 302. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes Hunter CSR #6576 email: rhunter@sftc.org.
